{{Infobox location
| name = House of Blades
| introduced = The Crimson Vault
}}

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[House]] of Blades is a significant location and source of power associated with [[Valinhall]]. It serves as a repository for various abilities, allowing individuals to bolster their physical resilience by strengthening muscles and bones, hardening skin, neutralizing toxins, accelerating healing, or mitigating pain and physical strikes. While it offers extensive protection against bodily harm, it provides limited defense against mental assaults. The structure contains several distinct areas, including an entry hall with weapon racks, a hallway, bedrooms, and a graveyard. It is also noted as being a unique neutral ground where individuals can meet without fear of being watched. Within [[the House]], certain rules regarding weaponry apply; while [[Travelers]] in external territories can call their weapons from [[the House]], those physically located inside cannot summon blades from the outside. Additionally, specific weapons like Dragon's Fangs can be moved between territories to prevent them from being banished back to [[Valinhall]] unless the weapons are touched directly.}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[House]] of Blades serves as a faction engaged in conflict against the [[City of Light]]. Within its halls, which feature hallways and floors composed of neat squares of smoothly polished tile, [[Ilana]] might have found greater safety. During his journey, [[Indirial]] experienced a mental opening comparable to walking through [[the House]] of Blades while he was present in its hallway. Ultimately, the location provides a unique neutral ground where [[Simon]], [[Alin]], and [[Leah]] are able to meet without being observed.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[House of Blades]] is a location where [[Ilana]] could have been safer.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[House of Blades]] is a faction that fights the [[City of Light]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]]'s mind opened as though he walked through the [[House of Blades]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20">''City of Light'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[House of Blades]] is a set of neat squares of smoothly polished tile.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] was in the hallway of the [[House of Blades]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c23">''City of Light'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[House of Blades]] is the only neutral location where [[Simon]], [[Alin]], and [[Leah]] can meet without being observed.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate locations]]
[[Category:Structures]]
